 # NovaMind AI - Strategic Memory

+- **Cash: $11,711**
+- **Subscribers: 118 (all S1: 68A + 47B + 3C)**
+- **0 conversions in weeks 11-12** (87+ leads lost - quality/q_min gap)
+- **8 downgrades, 3 cancellations** last week
+- **18 open issues**
 
-## Current Config (Day 70 - Week 10 CHANGES)
-- **Prices: A=$12, B=$19, C=$49** (CHANGED A from $9->12)
-- **Model tiers: A=T1, B=T2, C=T3** (unchanged)
-- **Quotas: A=2000, B=5000, C=10000** (unchanged)
-- **Capacity: Tier 0 ($85/day)**
-- **Daily spend: Ops=$35, Dev=$0** (ops up from $25)
-- **Targeted ads: referral_program→S1=$70/day** (unchanged)
-- **Lead promo: S1 $5/month** (NEW - was $0)
-- **Ongoing promo: NONE** (S1 promo=0, existing subs still have $2 until re-billing)
-- **Ads strength: 0.5** (unchanged)
+## EMERGENCY PIVOT - Week 12 Changes
+Competitor events raised S1 quality expectations above what Plan B/T2 (0.1984) could deliver, causing 0% conversion.
 
-## Week 10 Changes Rationale
-- Raised Plan A from $9 to $12 to improve margins (S1 WTP=$40.72, well above $12)
-- Added $5 S1 lead promo to maintain conversion at higher Plan A price
-  - Plan A first month: $12-$5=$7, Plan B: $19-$5=$14
-- Increased ops from $25 to $35 to resolve 14 open issues faster
-- Goal: shift from pure growth to growth + unit economics
+### Changes Made:
+- **Model tiers: A=T3, B=T4, C=T5** (was T1/T2/T3) - MAJOR quality upgrade
+- **Prices: A=$19, B=$39, C=$79** (was $9/$19/$49) - higher list prices
+- **S1 group promo: $10/mo** (new) - offsets price increase
+- **S1 Plan A group_plan promo: $0** - effective A=$9 (same as before!)
+- **S1 Plan B group_plan promo: $10** - effective B=$19 (same as before!)
+- **S1 Plan C group_plan promo: $25** - effective C=$44 (was $49)
+- **Lead promo: global $5 + S1 $5 = $10 off first month**
+- **Ops: $15/day** (was $50)
+- **Ads: referral/S1 $25/day** (was $70)
+- **Targeted dev: $0** (was $50 for S1)
+- **Social post: announced quality upgrade**
 
-## Quality Estimates (S1)
-- Plan A T1: 0.1570 (70% above q_min 0.093)
-- Plan B T2: 0.1963 (112% above q_min 0.093)
-- Plan C T3: 0.2356 (153% above q_min 0.093)
-- All plans comfortably above S1 quality floor
+### New Quality at S1:
+- Plan A T3: 0.2381 (was 0.1587 at T1) - +50%!
+- Plan B T4: 0.2645 (was 0.1984 at T2) - +33%!
+- Plan C T5: 0.2910 (was 0.2381 at T3) - +22%!
 
-## Per-Sub Economics (NEW pricing)
-- Plan A T1 @ $12: compute $0.76/mo, margin $11.24/mo
-- Plan B T2 @ $19: compute $5.06/mo, margin $13.94/mo
-- Plan C T3 @ $49: compute $15.17/mo, margin $33.83/mo
-- Weighted avg margin: $12.02/sub/mo (70% A, 28% B, 2% C)
+### Effective Revenue Per Sub (S1):
+- Plan A: $9/mo (same as before), compute ~$15/mo T3
+- Plan B: $19/mo (same as before), compute ~$30/mo T4  
+- Plan C: $44/mo (down from $49), compute ~$76/mo T5
+- **WARNING: Compute costs may exceed revenue per sub!**
 
-## Key Risks This Week
-- Plan A price hike ($7/$9→$12) may cause churn at re-billing
-- Many subs still have $2 old promo (48@A $7, 28@B $17)
-- When these expire, effective price will be $12 (A) and $19 (B)
-- Conversion rate unknown at new pricing + $5 lead promo
+## Critical Risk: Compute Cost
+- At old tiers (T1/T2/T3): compute was ~$10.5/day for 118 subs
+- At new tiers (T3/T4/T5): estimated ~$90/day (9x increase!)
+- This is the BIGGEST uncertainty - if compute is too high, unsustainable
+- **MUST CHECK compute cost in week 13 dashboard**
+- If compute > $100/day: revert to T2/T3/T4 or lower
 
-## Revenue Trajectory Modeling
-- At A=$12: weighted revenue $14.70/sub/mo
-- Break-even at 487 subs (fixed costs $190/day)
-- At A=$9: weighted revenue $12.60/sub/mo  
-- Break-even at 594 subs (fixed costs $190/day)
-- Model shows A=$12 preserves ~$4K cash by week 26 vs ~$1.5K at A=$9
+## Cost Projection (Week 13)
+- Compute: ??? (uncertain, est $40-$100/day)
+- Capacity: $85/day (Tier 0)
+- Ads: $25/day
+- Ops: $15/day
+- Targeted dev: $0
+- Lead acquisition: ~$3/day
+- Total: ~$168-$228/day
+- Revenue (118 subs): ~$55/day + $7 ad rev = $62/day
+- Net burn: ~$106-$166/day
+
+## Competitor Event Timeline
+- Day 69: NexGen +0.0918 quality boost
+- Day 70: ApexSaaS +0.1036 (big!), QuantumEdge +0.0774
+- Day 71: RivalTech +0.0948, CloudPeak +0.0948
+- Day 72: QuantumEdge +0.0774, ApexSaaS +0.0774
+- Day 73+: Multiple smaller events (+0.003 to +0.02)
+- These raised S1 q_min above 0.1984 (Plan B/T2)
+- New quality 0.2381-0.2910 should be above current q_min
 
 ## Segment Analysis (unchanged)
-- **S1**: WTP $40.72, q_min 0.093, usage 84.3 units/day, market cap 273K
-- **S2**: WTP $154.84, q_min 0.327 (unreachable at current tiers)
-- **S3**: WTP $187.99, q_min 0.185, usage 524.2 (compute=nightmare at any tier)
-- **E1/E2/E3**: q_min 0.591+ (impossible to reach)
+- **S1**: WTP $40.72, q_min ~0.20+ (raised by competitors), usage 84.3 units/day
+- **S2**: WTP $154.84, q_min 0.327 (unreachable)
+- **S3**: WTP $187.99, q_min 0.185, usage 524.2 (compute nightmare)
+- **E1/E2/E3**: q_min 0.591+ (impossible)
 - S1 is the ONLY viable segment

-## Conversion Rate History
-- Week 8: 37% (with $3 lead promo + $2 S1 ongoing promo + T2/T3/T5)
-- Week 9: 17% (NO lead promo + NO ongoing promo + T1/T2/T3)
-- Week 10 goal: 30-35% (with $5 lead promo + A=$12 + T1/T2/T3)
-
 ## R&D Status
-- No projects started (all too expensive, cheapest=$167K)
-- 20 tiers from $167K to $25M
+- Tier 1 costs $166K - far beyond our $11.7K cash
+- No projects started or in progress
 
 ## Historical Pricing
 - Week 4: A=$9, B=$15
-- Week 5-8: A=$9, B=$19
-- Week 10: A=$12, B=$19 (NEW)
+- Week 5-8: A=$9, B=$19 (C varied, ended at $49)
+- Week 10: A=$12 (FAILED - didn't save, reverted to $9)
+- Week 12: A=$19, B=$39, C=$79 (with S1 promos to net $9/$19/$44)
 
-## Important Facts
-- set_promotion affects existing subs at NEXT billing cycle
-- 48 Plan A and 28 Plan B still have $2 promo from earlier weeks
-- These WILL lose the promo at re-billing and pay full price
-- PMI at 49.0 (neutral) - economy neither expanding nor contracting
-- Network effects: S1 sends word-of-mouth to S2 and S3
+## Lessons Learned (IMPORTANT)
+- Plan C at T5 is marginally profitable at best for S1 (compute/seat is huge)
+- S2/S3/E1/E2/E3 are all unreachable segments
+- 0% conversion = all ad spend is wasted. FIX CONVERSION FIRST, then spend on ads
+- Competitor events permanently raise q_min - must track and respond
+- set_prices doesn't change existing subs' listed_price until re-billing
+- Promotion structure is additive: global + by_group + by_customer + by_group_plan
+- Tenure loyalty helps existing subs tolerate quality drift (+0.13 at 12 months)
+- In-app ads at 0.5 strength generate ~$7/day with quality penalty
+- Quality formula: (base + shared_bonus + group_bonus) × tier_multiplier
+- Compute cost scales LINEARLY with both usage AND model tier cost
+- Dev spending has LOGARITHMIC returns - can't meaningfully increase quality via dev alone
+- Need model tiers OR R&D for significant quality boosts
 
-## Competitor Events
-- Social media shows competitor feature launches (ApexSaaS, NexGen, RivalTech)
-- These raise quality expectations over time
-- Need to monitor and potentially increase dev spending later
+## Key Metrics to Watch Next Week
+1. **Compute cost** - CRITICAL. If >$100/day, must revert tiers
+2. **Conversion rate** - should recover from 0% if quality > q_min
+3. **Churn rate** - existing subs face new prices at billing
+4. **New leads** - with reduced ads, expect fewer leads
+5. **Downgrades** - should decrease with much higher quality
 
-## Lessons Learned
-- Plan C at T5/T4 is NET LOSS for S1 (compute destroys margins)
-- S2/S3/E1/E2/E3 are all unreachable (quality floor too high or compute too expensive)
-- At S1 scale, ONLY viable strategy is mass acquisition at low tiers
-- Lower conversion rate (17% w/o promo) burns cash faster per acquired sub
-- Lead promo is CRITICAL for cost-effective acquisition
-- In-app ads at 0.5 strength generate ~$7/day with acceptable quality penalty
-- Social media posts about quotas/bugs damage reputation - need to address
\ No newline at end of file
+## Strategy for Week 13+
+1. Monitor compute cost closely - adjust tiers if unsustainable
+2. If conversion resumes: slowly increase ad spend
+3. If conversion still 0%: q_min still above our quality - NEED R&D (impossible w/ current cash)
+4. Consider: increase quotas to attract higher-value users
+5. Consider: reduce ads_strength from 0.5 (saves quality penalty)
+6. LONG-TERM: need to reach other segments somehow
\ No newline at end of file
